understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by placing every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+' at the same time; if a level had multiple '+' tiles, all had to be occupied simultaneously for the engine to return WIN, and doing this across all levels would win the full run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement acted on the currently selected piece in 3-pixel grid steps (x/y changing by 3 per move) with x increasing to the right and y increasing downward. Walls and other objects could block movement, though the movement_blocked hints were only heuristic and didn’t guarantee failure. Some objects (e.g., blocks) were pushable if adjacent in the movement direction, shifting along with the selected piece when pushed, while walls/boundaries could stop motion. Control could be switched between multiple selectable pieces using CLICK on the exact pixel coordinates of the target selectable’s position.
